volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: The Captives (Section 2.2.355 - Section 3.1.461)

sections.label: Section 2.2.355
sections.original: atque utrumque .
sections.translation: and , indeed , both of them .

sections.label: Section 2.2.355b
sections.original: Di tibi omnis omnia optata offerant ,
sections.translation: May all the Gods grant you all your desires , since you have deigned me honor so great , and since you release me from my chains . Really , this is not so irksome now , since my neck is free from the collar-chain .

sections.label: Section 2.2.358
sections.original: Quod bonis bene fit beneficium , gratia ea gravida est bonis .
sections.translation: The kindnesses that are done to the good , thanks for the same are pregnant with blessings . Now , if you are about to send him thither , direct , instruct him , give him the orders

sections.label: Section 2.2.360
sections.original: quae ad patrem vis nuntiari . vin vocem huc ad te ?
sections.translation: which you wish to be carried to your father . Should you like me to call him to you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.360b
sections.original: Voca .
sections.translation: Do call him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.361
sections.original: Quae res bene vortat mihi meoque filio
sections.translation: May this affair turn out happily for myself and for my son , and for yourselves . ( To PHILOCRATES .) Your new master wishes you to pay faithful obedience to your former owner in what he wishes . For I have presented you to him , with the price of twenty minae set upon you :

sections.label: Section 2.3.365
sections.original: hic autem té ait mittere hinc velle ad patrem ,
sections.translation: and he says that he is desirous to send you away hence to his father , that he may there redeem my son , and that an exchange may be made between me and him for our respective sons .

sections.label: Section 2.3.368
sections.original: Vtroque vorsum rectumst ingenium meum ,
sections.translation: My disposition takes its course straight in either direction , both to yourself and to him ; as a wheel [*] you may make use of me ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.370
sections.original: vel ego huc vel illuc vortar , quo imperabitis .
sections.translation: either this way or that can I be turned , whichever way you shall command me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.371
sections.original: Tute tibi tuopte ingenio prodes plurumum ,
sections.translation: You yourself profit the most from your own disposition , when you endure slavery just as it ought to be endured . Follow me . ( To TYNDARUS .) See here ’ s your man .

sections.label: Section 2.3.373b
sections.original: Gratiám habeo tibi ,
sections.translation: I return you thanks , since you give me this opportunity and permission

sections.label: Section 2.3.375
sections.original: ut ego ad parentes hunc remittam nuntium ,
sections.translation: to send this messenger to my parents , who may relate all the matter in its order to my father , what I ’ m doing here , and what I wish to be done . ( To PHILOCRATES .) Now , Tyndarus , thus is it arranged between myself and him , that I ’ m to send you , valued at a fixed price , to my father in Elis ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.380
sections.original: si non rebitas huc , ut viginti minas
sections.translation: so that , if you don ’ t return hither , I ’ m to give twenty minae for you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.381b
sections.original: Recte convenisse sentio .
sections.translation: I think that you ’ ve come to a right understanding . For your father expects either myself or some messenger to come from here to him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.383b
sections.original: Ergo animum advortas volo
sections.translation: I wish you , then , to mind what message it is I want you to carry hence to my country to my father .

sections.label: Section 2.3.385
sections.original: Phílocrates , ut adhuc locorum feci , faciam sedulo ,
sections.translation: Philocrates , as up to this moment I have done , I will take all due care to endeavour that which may especially conduce to your interest , and to pursue the same with heart and soul , and with my ears .

sections.label: Section 2.3.388
sections.original: Facis ita ut te facere oportet . nunc animum advortas volo :
sections.translation: You act just as you ought to act ; now I wish you to give attention . In the first place of all , carry my respects to my mother and my father ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.390
sections.original: et cognatis et si quem alium benevolentem videris ;
sections.translation: and to my relations , and if any one else you see well-disposed towards me : say that I am in health here , and that I am a slave , in servitude to this most worthy man , who has ever honored me more and more with his respect , and does so still .

sections.label: Section 2.3.393
sections.original: Istuc ne praecipias , facile memoria memini tamen .
sections.translation: Don ’ t you be instructing me as to that ; I can , still , easily bear that in mind .

sections.label: Section 2.3.394
sections.original: Nam equidem , nisi quod custodem habeo , liberum me esse arbitror .
sections.translation: For , indeed , except that I have a keeper , I deem myself to be a free man .

sections.label: Section 2.3.395
sections.original: dicito patri , quo pacto mihi cum hoc convenerit
sections.translation: Tell my father on what terms I have agreed with this party about his son .

sections.label: Section 2.3.396b
sections.original: Quae memini , mora mera est monerier .
sections.translation: What I remember , it is sheer delay to be putting me in mind of .

sections.label: Section 2.3.397
sections.original: Vt eum redimat et remittat nostrum huc amborum vicem .
sections.translation: To redeem him , and to send him back here in exchange for both of us .

sections.label: Section 2.3.398
sections.original: Meminero .
sections.translation: I ’ ll remember it .

sections.label: Section 2.3.398b
sections.original: At quamprimum pote : istuc in rem utriquest maxime .
sections.translation: But as soon as he can that is especially to the interest of us both .

sections.label: Section 2.3.399
sections.original: Non tuom tu magis videre quam ille suom gnatum cupit .
sections.translation: You are not more anxious to see your son , than he is to see his .

sections.label: Section 2.3.400
sections.original: Meus mihi , suos cuique est carus .
sections.translation: My son is dear to myself , and his own to every man .

sections.label: Section 2.3.400b
sections.original: Numquid aliud vis patri
sections.translation: Do you wish any other message to be carried to your father ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.401b
sections.original: Me hic valere et ( tute audacter dicito ,
sections.translation: Say that I am well here ; and do you boldly tell him , Tyndarus , that we have been of dispositions for uninterrupted harmony between ourselves , and that you have neither been deserving of censure , nor that I have proved your enemy ; and that still , amid miseries so great , you have shown implicit obedience to your master ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.405
sections.original: neque med umquam deseruisse te neque factis neque fide ,
sections.translation: and that you have never abandoned me , either in deed or in fidelity , amid my wavering , unprosperous fortunes . When my father shall know this , Tyndarus , how well-disposed you have proved towards his son and himself , he will never be so avaricious but that he ’ ll give you your liberty for nothing . And by my own endeavours , if I return hence , I ’ ll make him do so the more readily .

sections.label: Section 2.3.410
sections.original: nam tua opera et comitate et virtute et sapientia
sections.translation: For by your aid and kindness , and good disposition and prudence , you have caused me to be allowed to return to my parents once again , inasmuch as to Hegio you have confessed both my rank and my wealth ; by means of which , through your wisdom , you have liberated your master from his chains .

sections.label: Section 2.3.414
sections.original: Feci ego ista út commemoras , et te meminisse id gratum est mihi .
sections.translation: The things which you mention I have done , and I am pleased that you remember this .

sections.label: Section 2.3.415
sections.original: merito tibi ea evenerunt a me ; nam nunc , Philocrates ,
sections.translation: Deservedly have they been done for you by me ; for now , Philocrates , if I , too , were to mention the things that you have kindly done for me , the night would cut short the day . For , had you been my slave even , no otherwise were you always obliging to me .

sections.label: Section 2.3.418b
sections.original: Di vostram fidem ,
sections.translation: Ye Gods , by our trust in you ! behold the kindly disposition of these persons ! How they draw the very tears from me !

sections.label: Section 2.3.420
sections.original: videas corde amare inter se . quantis lautus laudibus
sections.translation: See how cordially they love each other , and with what praises the servant has commended his master .

sections.label: Section 2.3.421b
sections.original: Pól istic me haud centesimam
sections.translation: I ’ troth , he hasn ’ t commended me the one hundredth part of what he himself deserves to be commended in my praises .

sections.label: Section 2.3.423
sections.original: Ergo cum optume fecisti , nunc adest occasio
sections.translation: Since , then , you have acted most becomingly , now there ’ s an opportunity to add to your good deeds in managing this matter with fidelity towards him .

sections.label: Section 2.3.425
sections.original: Magis non factum possum velle , quam opera experiar persequi ;
sections.translation: I am not able more to wish it done , than by my endeavours to try to bring it about . That you may know this , Hegio , with praises do I call supreme Jove to witness that I will not prove unfaithful to Philocrates [*] —

sections.label: Section 2.3.427b
sections.original: Probus es homo .
sections.translation: You are a worthy fellow .

sections.label: Section 2.3.428
sections.original: Nec me secus umquam ei facturum quicquam quam memet mihi .
sections.translation: And that I will never in anything act otherwise towards him than towards my own self .

sections.label: Section 2.3.429
sections.original: Istaec dicta te experiri et operis et factis volo ;
sections.translation: I wish you to put these speeches to the test , both by your deeds and your actions ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.430
sections.original: et , quo minus dixi quam volui de te , animum advortas volo ,
sections.translation: and inasmuch as I have said the less about you than I had wished , I wish you the more to give me your attention , and take you care not to be angry with me by reason of these words . But , I beseech you , reflect that you are sent hence home with a price set upon you at my risk , and that my life is here left as a pledge for you . Do not you forget me the very moment that you have left my presence ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.435
sections.original: quom me servom in servitute pro ted hic reliqueris ,
sections.translation: since you will have left me here behind a captive in captivity for yourself , and don ’ t consider yourself as free , and forsake your pledge [*] , and not use your endeavours for you to bring his son home again , in return for me . Understand that you are sent hence valued at twenty minae . Take care to prove scrupulously faithful ; take care that you show not a wavering fidelity .

sections.label: Section 2.3.440
sections.original: nam pater , scio , faciet quae illum facere oportet omnia ;
sections.translation: For my father , I am sure , will do everything that he ought to do . Preserve me as a constant friend to you , and find out [*] this person so lately discovered . These things , by your right hand , holding you with my own right hand , do I beg of you ; do not prove less true to me than I have proved to you . This matter do you attend to ; you are now my master , you my patron , you my father ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.445
sections.original: tibi commendo spes opesque meas .
sections.translation: to you do I commend my hopes and my fortunes .

sections.label: Section 2.3.445b
sections.original: Mandavisti satis .
sections.translation: You have given injunctions enough . Are you satisfied if I bring back accomplished what you have enjoined ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.446b
sections.original: Satis .
sections.translation: Satisfied .

sections.label: Section 2.3.447
sections.original: Et tua et tua huc ornatus reveniam ex sententia .
sections.translation: According to your wishes , and ( to TYNDARUS ) according to yours , will I return hither provided . Is there anything else ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.448b
sections.original: Vt quam primum possis redeas .
sections.translation: For you to return back as soon as ever you can .

sections.label: Section 2.3.448c
sections.original: Res monet .
sections.translation: The business itself reminds me of that .

sections.label: Section 2.3.449
sections.original: Sequere me , viaticum ut dem á trapezita tibi ,
sections.translation: Follow me , that I may give you your expenses for the journey at my banker ’ s ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.450
sections.original: eadem opera a praetore sumam syngraphum .
sections.translation: on the same occasion I ’ ll get a passport from the Praetor .

sections.label: Section 2.3.450b
sections.original: Quem syngraphum ?
sections.translation: What passport [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.451
sections.original: Quem hic ferat secum ad legionem , hinc ire huic ut liceat domum .
sections.translation: For him to take with him hence to the army , that he may be allowed to go home from here . ( To TYNDARUS .) You go in-doors .

sections.label: Section 2.3.452b
sections.original: Bene ambulato .—
sections.translation: Speed you well .

sections.label: Section 2.3.452c
sections.original: Bene vale .
sections.translation: Right heartily , farewell .

sections.label: Section 2.3.452d
sections.original: Edepol rem meam
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I compassed my design , when I purchased these men of the Quaestors out of the spoil . I have released my son from slavery , if so it pleases the Gods ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.455
sections.original: at etiam dubitavi , hos homines emerem an non emerem , diu .
sections.translation: and yet I hesitated a long time whether I should purchase or should not purchase these persons . Watch that man indoors , if you please , you servants , that he may nowhere move a foot without a guard . I shall soon make my appearance at home ; now I ’ m going to my brother ’ s , to see my other captives ; at the same time I ’ ll enquire whether any one knows this young man .

sections.label: Section 2.3.460
sections.original: sequere tu , te ut amittam ; ei rei primum praevorti volo .—
sections.translation: ( To PHILOCRATES .) Do you follow , that I may despatch you . I wish attention first to be paid to that matter .

sections.label: Section 3.1.461
sections.original: Míser homo est , qui ipse sibi quod edit quaerit et id aegre invenit ,
sections.translation: Wretched is that man who is in search of something to eat , and finds that with difficulty ; but more wretched is he who both seeks with difficulty , and finds nothing at all ; most wretched is he , who , when he desires to eat , has not that which he may eat . But , by my faith , if I only could , I ’ d willingly tear out the eyes of this day ;
